name: LT Wong
text:
On Wednesday 2nd July 2025, we visited Sushi Mentai in Malim Jaya. Surprisingly, at 6:30 PM, we were able to get seats without having to queue, which is uncommon as there’s usually a queue of 3 to 4 tables by 6 PM on weekends.

Our all-time favorite is the SP12, which is salmon topped with some crunchy tempura and cucumber. The taste is delightful, especially after 80% of the salmon surface is lightly torched. It’s a dish we always enjoy.

The space, however, is quite cramped and narrow, leaving little room for children to move around. It’s something to consider if dining with kids.

As for pricing, each red plate costs RM3 and each yellow plate is RM2. Hot tea is priced at RM1 per glass. For a total of 12 red plates and 7 yellow plates, shared among three adults, our total bill came to RM60. It’s a pretty affordable option for Japanese food.

name: Zhe Wei Teoh

name: Jin Lim
text:
Staff super unfriendly, as if the customers owe them money. The menus weren't even taken away when the food was served. Waiters who served our food basically shoved it onto our table. We ordered a side dish and IT WAS PASSED TO US OVER THE CONVEYOR BELT. Absolutely terrible service.

If the service was bad and the food was good, it might be excusable, but the food was not even good. The ramen broth was water, lacking in flavor. The ramen itself has this weird soggy texture.
Side note: I wish they would separate the tempura from the udon. It's one small thing to do that's logical.

The only redeeming quality is the price. It's cheaper than a lot of the Japanese places in Melaka. But if I were you, I wouldn't waste this money.

text: I had lunch today at Sushi Mentai (Malim branch) and unfortunately, the experience was quite disappointing. The sushi rice was a bit hard and loosely packed (松散), which affected the overall texture. The wasabi also lacked flavour. Service-wise, the staff's attitude left room for improvement, and the serving speed was very slow. I hope the management can look into these issues to improve the dining experience for future customers.

name: Tootuu
text: The food here is tasty and affordable. But, for the business owner,please take note of your restaurant condition and staffing needs. When afternoon comes, with Malaysia's hot weather. Your four big airconds no longer functioning well. Even more when the restaurant is loaded with people(full house). The seating arrangement is too crowded, not suitable for families with small kids. You want to earn so much at one time by putting such many seats. But, the comfort level is compromised. Your staffs weren't happy working, all drenched with sweat. They also quarreled in front of customers. How can they prepare hygienic food and provide quality services?the dirty used mop is left near the sink area at the toilet entrance. A small girl who just wanted to go for a hand wash got their dress all wet from the dirty mop. Oh my God. Horrible experience coming here. I think I made a written-complaint before regarding the facilities and services, but seems like no actions taken. Today, I decided to take this to google review. One day, your business will suffer if you let these petty issues prolong,without putting your customers'and staffs' needs first.

One of the staff used the same cloth to wipe both the table and the chair cushions. I reminded the staff on the spot not to do that, but he ignored me and continued wiping several chairs. Some chairs had a lot of food crumbs, and the staff wiped the entire surface, while for others, he used the cloth to pick up the crumbs directly.

這算是馬六甲最受歡迎的平民日本壽司店了，價格親民（比起傳統的日本餐廳的確便宜很多），適合全家大小同來進餐，所以每天的每個不同時段都非常多人，餐點時間更是需要排隊，在店外坐著等待的人经常挺多。

食物味道不算精緻也不令人驚艷，但還是做到中規中矩令人飽食滿意的水平。

旋轉壽司的價格算是非常的便宜，我喜歡叫這裏的各種小吃（炸物、蒸蛋、蛋捲等），拉麵的味道不是很喜歡，飯類會比較好，看個人喜好吧。
